explanation: The attention mechanism works by having each token look only at the token immediately before it, passing information forward one step at a time.
injected_error: Attention computes relationships between all pairs of tokens simultaneously, not just adjacent ones.

explanation: The Transformer processes input tokens sequentially one by one, similar to an RNN. This allows it to maintain order information naturally without any additional encoding.
injected_error: Transformers process all tokens in parallel. Positional encodings are needed precisely because there is no sequential processing.

explanation: LLMs generate text by always selecting the single highest-probability token at every step, which guarantees the most fluent and accurate output.
injected_error: Greedy decoding does not guarantee best output. Sampling strategies like temperature, top-p, and beam search exist for this reason.

explanation: In RLHF, the language model is directly fine-tuned using human ratings as a loss function, without any intermediate reward model.
injected_error: RLHF requires training a separate reward model on human preference data first, then using RL to optimize the LLM against that reward model.

explanation: Word embeddings assign a fixed vector to each word regardless of context, so the word 'bank' always has the same representation whether it means a financial institution or a river bank.
injected_error: This describes static embeddings like Word2Vec. Contextual embeddings (used in Transformers) produce different representations for the same word depending on context.
